description: Ensure resident profile photos display prominently next to names on the Carer Dashboard and Task Board
steps: In frontend/src/pages/dashboards/CarerDashboard.tsx, the photo is already rendered next to names (around line 1017-1025) using `residentPhoto((res as any)?.photo_url)`. Since FEAT-001 adds photo_url to the Resident type, update the cast from `(res as any)?.photo_url` to `res?.photo_url`. Also increase the photo avatar size from 28x28 to 36x36 for better visibility. Add a subtle box-shadow to make photos more prominent.

In frontend/src/pages/TaskBoard.tsx, add resident photos next to names in the task board rows. Find the resident info section that renders the name via `<Link to={/residents/${rid}} ...>{first.resident_name}</Link>`. Before the name, add a photo avatar (or initials fallback) similar to CarerDashboard. Use the residents list from the useTasks response which includes resident_id. Add a useResidents hook call and display photos using the same `residentPhoto()` helper pattern. Import useResidents from hooks and the BACKEND constant pattern for photo URLs.
Ensure the photo displays consistently: 36px circle with object-fit:cover, 2px solid var(--border) border, and a fallback to initials in a colored circle when no photo is available.
acceptance_criteria: Resident profile photos appear as 36px circular avatars next to resident names on the Carer Dashboard
Resident profile photos appear as 36px circular avatars next to resident names on the Task Board (/tasks page)
When no photo is uploaded, initials are shown in a styled circle as fallback
Photos are fetched using the correct backend URL prefix
findings: Successfully added 36px circular photo avatars with box-shadow to both CarerDashboard and TaskBoard. Removed (res as any) cast in CarerDashboard since photo_url is now on the Resident type. Added useResidents hook and residentPhoto helper to TaskBoard. All 19 pre-existing TS errors in other files (CareNotes, Emar, Incidents, Training) remain unrelated to this change.
